page: Amazon Web Services c8a.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs utilizing the x86_64 architecture, driven by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor operating at 4.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, yielding a 1:2 core-to-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps.

Benchmark data positions this instance in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance. The AMD EPYC processor achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2819.0 and a multi-core score of 31526.0, alongside a PassMark CPU score of 139164.12. Memory performance is highly competitive, with cached read-only bandwidth reaching 16796.25 GB/sec and uncached large-block write bandwidth at 190.63 GB/sec. The instance also demonstrates top-tier capabilities in cryptographic operations, database transactions, and static web serving, the latter reaching a throughput of 107.42 GB/sec.

While the c8a.24xlarge excels in raw compute and memory bandwidth, it presents specific tradeoffs. The absence of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network storage, which may impact I/O-heavy workloads. Additionally, while LLM prompt processing speeds are in the top tier across small, medium, and large models, text generation for smaller models falls into the average tier at 233.38 tokens/sec. This instance is highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy, non-GPU workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, high-throughput web serving, and large-scale database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services c8a.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps. Benchmark results place the c8a.24xlarge in the top tier for single-core and multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic operations. It is highly efficient for compute-intensive tasks, including software compilation, database operations, and static web serving. However, the lack of local storage requires network-attached alternatives, and text generation for smaller LLM models is average compared to its top-tier prompt processing speeds.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and 40 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier CPU and memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and 40 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-frequency, compute-optimized virtual server featuring ninety-six dedicated AMD EPYC cores and high-speed DDR5 memory for intensive parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz
192.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic performance
40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Optimal for software compilation, database operations, and static web serving
